name: SpeedRunners
short_description: Cut-throat multiplayer running game that pits 4 players against each other, locally and/or online. Run, jump, swing around, and use devious weapons and pick-ups to knock opponents off-screen! One of the most competitive games you'll ever play.
detailed_description:
In a city filled with superheroes, getting to crimes becomes a competition on its own. Fortunately there are enough rockets, bombs, grappling hooks, spikes, and other goodies lying around -- to make the competition fun and fast. Welcome to SpeedRunners.

SpeedRunners is a 4 player competitive platformer with grappling hooks, power-ups, and interactive environments. Run, jump, swing around, fire rockets, grapple onto people to knock them off screen.

Key Features

  • 4 Player Competitive Platforming!

  • Keep up with the fastest player in-game, or fall offscreen and get eliminated!

  • Use weapons like missiles, mines and grappling hooks to screw over your friends, and make new enemies!

  • Local and online multiplayer! Can you have 2 players on a couch against 2 other players on the internet? Yes of course!

  • Bots enabled - play on your own with several AI opponents. Or put them into matches with other people!

  • A tutorial teaches you how to play

  • Controller compatible (and recommended!)

  • A dozen expertly designed maps

  • Unique game modifiers

  • A character named Unic!

  • Full blown level editor with over 10k user created levels

  • An exciting single player campaign

  • Create your own custom in-game trails (visible when you hit super-speed)

supported_languages:
English*, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Czech, Romanian, Russian, Simplified Chinese, Dutch, Hungarian, Greek, Norwegian, Portuguese - Brazil, Turkish, Ukrainian, Polish, Japanese, Korean
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:
SpeedRunners Review: The 1,000-Hour Grind and the Veteran Wall
​I’ve put nearly 1,000 hours into SpeedRunners, and after all that time, I can say it’s a great game, but it has two very different sides—especially lately.
​The Good: Low-Spec Frenzy and Pure Addiction
​The best thing about SpeedRunners is that it’s a truly good, high-octane game. The sense of speed is incredible. One of its biggest strengths is being a low-spec game; you don't need a powerful PC for it to run like lightning, making it super accessible. It’s the kind of game you can play every single day without even realizing how much time has passed. The mix of running, jumping, using the grappling hook, and timing your items is extremely fun and keeps you hooked as you try to perfect every lap.
​The Bad: The Skill Wall and Limited Matchmaking
​However, the downside is that since the game has been out for years, the community has changed. Currently, there is a lack of new players, which has created a serious issue. You run into players who have been around for years, and the average skill level is way above what it should be.
​This makes the ranked matchmaking feel very limited. Because the player base is small, the system constantly matches you against the same people. The frustrating part is that these players often have a lower rank than they deserve. They might look like rookies based on their badge, but they actually play much better because they’ve been dedicated to the game for a long time. It’s hard to compete fairly when you’re constantly facing these veterans in disguise, making ranking up feel more like a punishment than an achievement.
​Final Verdict
​For a new player, SpeedRunners can be intimidating because of this massive skill gap. But if you're looking for a fast, fun platformer-racer that doesn't eat up your PC resources, the core gameplay is amazing. If you have the patience to learn while losing to the same "experts" over and over, there’s a lot of fun to be had.

lit game, hard to get into, but once you get into it it's great (me at 2019)

this game is destroying my fingers (me at 2025)
